Kael lies on the ground, wordless. Not dead — emptied. Every stolen word shaken loose by your assault, scattering into the network like sparks. Some will find their way home. Some won't.

He looks at his palm. STORM is gone. Underneath, faintly, SEED flickers. Then goes dark.

"You took my storm," he says. He doesn't sound angry. He sounds relieved. "I'm nothing again."

"You're Seed again," you say. "That's not nothing."

He closes his eyes.

The network survives. Damaged. Changed. But surviving. Like everyone in it.

ending_mercy.title: The Mercy Ending
ending_mercy.text:
"Give them back," you say. "Every word."

Kael stares at you. The hurricane screams around him.

"You don't understand," he says. "They gave them to me. They chose this. I can't just—"

"They chose it because they were dying and you were the only one who would take their pain. That's not consent. That's desperation."

The words hang in the storm. Kael's eyes are wet.

"I was trying to help," he whispers. The storm-voice is gone. It's just him. Just a boy who ate too many people's pain.

"I know," you say. "But help that eats people isn't help. It's hunger wearing a kind face."

Luma steps forward. Two Seeds facing each other across a hurricane.

"I water dead words," she says to Kael. "Every day. They don't grow. But they don't die either. That's what Seed does. We hold potential. We don't consume it."

Kael looks at her. At his own palm, where STORM covers SEED. At the orbiting words — dozens of consumed souls.

"If I give them back," he says, "I'll be Seed again. Potential forever. The thing that hasn't happened yet."

"Yes," you say.

"That's a prison."

"It's a choice."

The longest pause of your life.

Then Kael opens his hands. And the storm opens with them.

Words pour out of him like light from a broken lantern — Echo and Pulse and Thread and Light, a hundred stolen words finding their way back into the network, flooding the dying roots with reconnection. The First Root blazes. The network surges.

Kael falls. Small again. His palm reads SEED. Just SEED.

Luma catches him. She's the only one strong enough — Seed holding Seed.

"Welcome back," she says.

He cries. Not storm-tears. Just tears. The kind that water things.

ending_sacrifice.title: The Sacrifice Ending
ending_sacrifice.text:
"Ren."

One word. They know.

They've always known. Since the Hollow Hall. Since the Echo Chamber where their past self confessed to trying to save everyone. Since the bridge, where they felt the connections snapping.

Ren steps forward. What's left of them — a flicker, an outline, two visible fingers and a voice that comes from slightly the wrong place.

"I couldn't save them before," they say. "The Hollow Court. My friends. I let them fade because I was afraid of becoming what Kael is."

They look at you. Through you. Their hollow eyes hold every word they ever absorbed, every person they ever tried to save.

"I'm not afraid anymore."

Ren opens their arms. HOLLOW blazes on their palm — brighter than you've ever seen it, brighter than it should be possible for someone so faded.

And they absorb.

Not a word. Not a bolt. The storm. The whole storm. Every stolen word, every crackling fragment, every syllable of every soul Kael ever consumed — Ren takes it all. Into the hollow. Into the space where a person used to be.

Kael screams. Seri screams. Luma hides her face.

Ren doesn't scream. They glow. For one perfect, terrible moment, Ren is the fullest thing in the network. Every word they absorbed makes them solid — completely, beautifully solid. You can see their face clearly for the first time. Young. Kind. Whole.

They look at you and smile.

"I can feel it," they say. "Everything. All of them. They're all in here."

Then the light goes out.

Ren is gone. Not faded — gone. The hollow filled itself completely and closed. Every stolen word, held safe inside a person who finally held enough.

Kael lies on the ground, empty, wordless. SEED flickers on his palm. He's a child again.

The network steadies. Not healed — held. Ren is the bridge now. Ren is the junction. Ren is the connection between everything Kael broke.

"They wanted to feel something real one more time," Seri whispers, tears running down her translucent face. "They did."

Luma puts a flower on the spot where Ren was standing. It's the first thing that's ever grown in the deep root.

You stand in the silence that Ren made, and you know: some people don't get to stay. Some people become the thing they save.
